INTRO





Hey, as I mentioned, I'm Andrew Lambert. I live with my beautiful wife, Jeannie and our four kids out here in Hanover, Maryland. I'm an award-winning research scientist.



You may have heard about some of my pioneering health research online or in the news like my recent work on complex health biomarkers and aetiology, but despite my successes, nothing was more baffling to me than my own wife Jeanie's health…



STORY



And this is going to sound familiar, Jeanie was 37 when our fourth child, Dawn was born and already 42 pounds overweight.

UNIQUE INGREDIENT

One found it supports healthy cholesterol levels by 12.5%. But more importantly, research published in the respected Journal of Functional 





UNIQUE MECHANISM



Foods and the University of Milan found purple Maqui  Berry Peel instantly triggers something called mitochondrial biogenesis, which is kind of the geeky way of saying it quickly increases the number of something found in every cell in our body called mitochondria.



I know that word may sound sciencey to you, but let me make you an expert in just one minute. 



The mitochondria are the power plants of your cells. They're vital to life-producing energy for everything your body does, and you have a lot of it - trillions. In fact, from the moment you were a growing baby all the way to now, your mitochondria have been working hard to power you from your muscles to your digestion, to your liver, even your thoughts. They keep your skin young and healthy and your heart beats strong whether you're sleeping, working, or at the gym. 



In fact, it takes 1 billion mitochondria to power just one beat of your heart. Researchers always thought that as we all share the same DNA, we all had the same number of mitochondria, but in early 2006, scientists used cutting edge microscopic techniques to measure the number of mitochondria on 1700 American women and made a little known breakthrough.



Adults actually have very different amounts of mitochondria. 



We didn't know what this meant until 10 years later when in 2016 a team of seven scientists from Harvard revisited the same 1700 women and stumbled upon a bizarre discovery. Every single woman who had a low number of mitochondria 10 years ago was now overweight with an average 44 pounds more fat on their arms, back, face, and legs. And, get this, every single woman with a high number of mitochondria 10 years ago was still slim, toned and lean and healthy and had not put on a single pound. 



They found that just a small 5% increase of mitochondria meant a whopping 17 pounds less fat, a 2.5 inch slimmer waist, less oxidative stress and less leptin, which is the hunger hormone. And those with more mitochondria had smooth young skin, sported excellent blood sugar, blood pressure and cholesterol levels, had almost no digestive problems and even higher dopamine levels (That's the feel-good hormone).



More incredibly, they found zero difference in the calories eaten or exercise between the lean and obese women over 10 years, and that's exactly why they named mitochondria our tiny slimming furnaces. 



Then in 2017, a team from the University of Florida College of Medicine went one step further and tested genetically increasing mitochondria count on obese mice. 



The results? 



While being fed the exact same diet and doing the exact same amount of activity…



This is the mouse with low mitochondria count and this is the mouse with high mitochondria count. 



These mice lost over 60% of their fat mass in just eight weeks. The high mitochondria mouse not only had 30% less blood sugar and 37% less bad cholesterol, but research has found even by increasing their high fat, high sugar diet, they were immune to weight gain.



And my research didn't stop there. 



[Scene Change: Andrew Reporting from his Car]

I'm on my way back from the library and I just found another brand new study from the acclaimed American Diabetes Association that looked at 26 pairs of twins where one twin was a obese and the other one was lean, and check this out:



Although these twins were identical in every way, the researchers found just one key difference. Each lean twin had 25% more mitochondria than the obese twin. They published their results in the Journal of Diabetes and they reported that the twins with high mitochondria had 39 pounds less fat and 75% less visceral fat (which is the dangerous cancer causing deep fat around their liver and organs). They were insulin sensitive and even their fat cells were half the size of the obese twin. Crazy, right? 



[Back to Andrew In Normal Narration Mode and on Screen]



Over the next days, I uncovered 12 more studies buried deep in the internet and from prestigious medical journals like the American Journal of Clinical Nutrition and Frontiers in Bioscience all proving that when the number of your mitochondria is low, your metabolism is slow. That's because while scientists now universally agree that having low mitochondrial levels is the trigger for not just obesity but almost all ageing related diseases and conditions up to 10 years before they arise, like diabetes, stroke, memory loss, kidney disease, muscle loss, frail bones, hearing loss, and even depression, having high mitochondria is the opposite.



It's like a cheat code to ultimate health because like mini slimming furnaces, mitochondria work 24/7 inside every cell in your body, taking your fat stores and the food you eat - that's sugars, fats and proteins - and mixing it with the air you breathe to create natural energy. And this isn't caffeine like fake stimulant energy, but real clean and pure energy. The sort of energy you feel after a long restful sleep where you feel warm and confident and youthful. The same clean energy your body craves, where you'll find it's already 10:00 PM and you're still out with your friends or watching a romantic movie with your spouse without falling asleep. And the more mitochondria you have, the more fat you dissolve and energy you create to power you and your organs correctly and healthily. And I probably don't have to tell you this, but healthy organs means longevity.



Just imagine tricking your body into thinking it's running a marathon, but you're not. You're relaxing on the couch watching tv, and then you get an idea of how powerful increasing mitochondria is for feeling incredible, smoothing out skin and of course burning stubborn fat. 



And you keep burning calories like a furnace all night, all day, week after week, year after year with no rebounding or side effects. 



Remember the Harvard study that found for every 5% more mitochondria you have means a huge 17 pounds less fat? 



Well get this: A study from the leading institute for biomedical research in Italy looked at men and women from Ulassai and discovered something remarkable. They have not 5%, but a huge 50% more mitochondria than the average American, Brit or Canadian. 



So it's no wonder the Ulassai residents who eat a diet of starchy carbs like pasta and bread and heavy fats like butter, fried food and cheese, still look so young, have strong slender bodies, healthy hearts and arteries, smooth digestion and live to very old ages…



Or why studies found that Ulassai residents are immune to weight gain. 



But don't start booking a flight to Ulassai to harvest some mitochondria boosting purple Maqui  berry peel yourself - because that's not the full story.





INGREDIENTS 





I also uncovered that they add the special tangy herb called Rhodiola to every dinner. 



Locals call Rhodiola the golden herb, as to them it's as precious as gold. 



I can see why. 



It's overflowing with more than 140 active polyphenols and vitamins. It's an adaptogen, which means it helps your body in reducing stress, improving mood and concentration, and supporting memory and brain health. 



One study here from German scientists found that eating daily Rhodiola improves mental performance by up to 20% and helps you sleep deeper. Simply put, it makes you and your brain happy. And the best part? Studies in the prestigious journals Phytomedicine and Cellular Longevity found Rhodiola quickly increases healthy calorie burning mitochondria as well. 



Can you imagine the metabolism boosting results combining purple Maqui  berry peel and rhodiola herb together? 



But it doesn't stop there because next was Haematococcus. An unusual algae found native in the freshwater lakes around Ulassai.



Unlike green algae, Haematococcus is bright red. That's because it's crammed full of the mighty red antioxidant astaxanthin that, in 2023, the University of Toyama in Japan found speedily multiplies mitochondria as well. 



It's also 500 times more powerful than vitamin E, another well-known antioxidant, at flushing out dangerous free radicals - unstable molecules in your body that can increase the risk of disease. 



Finally, I found Amla, a unique and rare sour fruit, one of the richest sources of polyphenols and flavonoids in the world. 



Amla is nature's gut cleanser. It supports healthy digestion by improving the gut's good bacteria. It reduces bloating, aids, regular bowel movements and lessens heartburn. 



It's also great for your hair. One study of 42 people with hair loss found that daily Amla blocked an enzyme involved with hair loss, increasing hair growth, density and thickness. And famous Japanese scientists found that yes, you guessed it, Amla increases fat shrinking mitochondria too. 



Finally, it all made sense. We all lose mitochondria at different speeds based on our age and even just random chance.



The same way genetically identical twins can have different amounts of mitochondria like the study from earlier, so can you, your family and your friends.



And I'm sure you have that one annoying friend who eats what they want and stays skinny, but you eat a single piece of pizza or cake and it instantly sticks right to your belly. They don't have a secret. These studies prove they're just lucky to have more mitochondria than you. 



The average American loses 2% of mitochondria every year. It may not sound like a lot, but after 35 years old, this speeds up and by 70 years old, many have half the mitochondria they had when they were young. 



Remember eating what you want as a teenager with no issues, but you seem to hit a brick wall as you get older?



That's why.
